display.purpose: Hero headline only — one per screen, setup view only.

headline.purpose: Modal and overlay titles. Module names in processing cards.

title.purpose: Panel headings, chat topbar, summary section titles.

body.purpose: All descriptive copy and UI text. Base for reading views.

label.purpose: Metadata: file names, timestamps, section markers, source tags. Minimum size tier — never go below.

shadows.name: ambient-ground
shadows.purpose: Cards and modals at rest. Two-layer: atmospheric + definition. Grounds without lifting.

shadows.name: accent-hover-glow
shadows.purpose: Module cards and interactive surfaces on hover. Warmth, not height.

shadows.name: focus-ring
shadows.purpose: Keyboard focus indicator on inputs and interactive controls.

motion.name: ease-out
motion.purpose: Default easing for all state transitions. Use everywhere.

motion.name: ease-spring-prohibited
motion.purpose: PROHIBITED — produces a bounce. Replace with ease-out across the codebase.

motion.name: duration-short
motion.purpose: Micro-interactions: opacity, color changes, icon fills.

motion.name: duration-standard
motion.purpose: Standard state transitions: borders, backgrounds, transforms.

motion.name: duration-entrance
motion.purpose: Panel entrances, slideUp, fadeIn. Upper limit for user-triggered animations.

breakpoints.name: mobile
breakpoints.note: Single-column layout. Setup screen padding reduces.

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: The single most important action on a screen. Study Flame background — use at most once per view.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { min-height: 42px; padding: 0 20px; border-radius: 10px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 600; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #ffffff; background: #C2410C; border: none; cursor: pointer; transition: transform 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1), box-shadow 0.2s ease, filter 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-primary:hover { transform: translateY(-1px); box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(194,65,12,0.22); filter: brightness(1.08); } .ds-btn-primary:active { transform: scale(0.97) translateY(0); filter: brightness(0.96); } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(194,65,12,0.09); border: 1px solid rgba(194,65,12,0.32); } .ds-btn-primary:disabled { opacity: 0.38; cursor: default; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: Supporting actions — cancel, secondary navigation, non-destructive operations. Stays in plane.
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { min-height: 42px; padding: 0 16px; border-radius: 10px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.875rem; color: #1C1917; background: #F4F0EB; border: 1px solid #DDD8D2; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s ease, border-color 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #EDE8E2; border-color: rgba(194,65,12,0.32); transform: translateY(-1px); } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: none; box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(194,65,12,0.09); border-color: rgba(194,65,12,0.32); } .ds-btn-secondary:disabled { opacity: 0.38; cursor: default; pointer-events: none; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Single-line text entry. Recessed into the surface at rest; accent ring activates on focus only.
components.css: .ds-input-group { display: grid; gap: 7px; } .ds-input-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.64rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #78716C; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #FAFAF8; border: 1px solid #DDD8D2; border-radius: 9px; color: #1C1917; padding: 10px 14px; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; font-size: 0.9rem; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: rgba(194,65,12,0.32); box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px rgba(194,65,12,0.09); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #78716C; }

components.name: Module Card
components.description: Clickable study module tile on the dashboard. Lifts 2px on hover with accent warmth.
components.css: .ds-module-card { position: relative; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 10px; padding: 20px 18px 16px; background: #F4F0EB; border: 1px solid #DDD8D2; border-radius: 14px; color: #1C1917; cursor: pointer; text-align: left; width: 100%; transition: border-color 0.2s ease, box-shadow 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-module-card::after { content: '→'; position: absolute; bottom: 15px; right: 15px; color: #78716C; font-size: 0.9rem; transition: color 0.2s ease, transform 0.2s ease; } .ds-module-card:hover { border-color: rgba(194,65,12,0.32); box-shadow: 0 4px 16px rgba(194,65,12,0.22); transform: translateY(-2px); } .ds-module-card:hover::after { color: #C2410C; transform: translateX(3px); } .ds-module-card__tag { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.62rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #C2410C; } .ds-module-card__title { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; font-size: 1rem; font-weight: 600; color: #1C1917; line-height: 1.25; padding-right: 24px; } .ds-module-card__meta { font-size: 0.84rem; color: #78716C; }

components.name: Chip (Accent)
components.description: Active module indicator, source tags, and any state-marking label that needs to stand out.
components.css: .ds-chip-accent { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; gap: 7px; border-radius: 999px; background: rgba(194,65,12,0.09); border: 1px solid rgba(194,65,12,0.32); padding: 5px 12px; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.64rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #C2410C; white-space: nowrap; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; }

components.name: Chat Bubble — User
components.description: User message in the Q&A chat. Accent-tinted; aligned right.
components.html:
Du
Was ist ein Eigenvektor?
components.css: .ds-chat-msg { display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 4px; max-width: 600px; } .ds-chat-msg--user { align-self: flex-end; align-items: flex-end; } .ds-chat-label { font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.59rem; letter-spacing: 0.08em; text-transform: uppercase; color: #78716C; } .ds-chat-bubble { padding: 12px 15px; border-radius: 14px; line-height: 1.72; font-size: 0.9rem; font-family: 'Inter', system-ui, sans-serif; word-break: break-word; border: 1px solid #DDD8D2; } .ds-chat-bubble--user { background: rgba(194,65,12,0.09); border-color: rgba(194,65,12,0.32); color: #1C1917; }

components.name: Chat Bubble — Agent
components.description: Agent response in the Q&A chat. Neutral panel; supports nested Lora headings for structured answers.
components.html:
Study Agent
Ein Eigenvektor ist ein Vektor, der durch eine lineare Transformation nur skaliert, nicht rotiert wird.
components.css: .ds-chat-msg--agent { align-self: flex-start; align-items: flex-start; } .ds-chat-bubble--agent { background: #F4F0EB; border-color: #DDD8D2; color: #1C1917; } .ds-chat-bubble--agent strong { color: #1C1917; font-weight: 600; } .ds-chat-bubble--agent h2, .ds-chat-bubble--agent h3 { font-family: 'Lora', Georgia, serif; color: #1C1917; line-height: 1.3; margin-top: 0.75rem; }

components.name: Source Tag
components.description: Citation pill below agent responses. Identifies which uploaded file the answer draws from.
components.css: .ds-source-tag { display: inline-block; font-family: 'JetBrains Mono', monospace; font-size: 0.59rem; color: #C2410C; background: rgba(194,65,12,0.09); border: 1px solid rgba(194,65,12,0.32); padding: 3px 8px; border-radius: 999px; max-width: 240px; overflow: hidden; text-overflow: ellipsis; white-space: nowrap; }

narrative.overview:
Study Agent's design inhabits the feeling of a well-used academic textbook: warm paper, precise serif type, terracotta ink in the margins, and the quiet authority of something trusted through repeated use. The interface does not announce itself. It recedes so the material can speak — creating the conditions for concentration rather than competing with the content.

The warmth is structural, not decorative. It comes from typography (Lora's humanist curves), from color (parchment neutrals tinted toward ochre, a terracotta accent used sparingly), and from spacing (breathing room that mirrors the margins of a real book). There are no gradients-as-branding, no glowing accents, no choreographed animation sequences. The system is precisely restrained: every element does exactly its job.

This system explicitly rejects: the AI-Tech-Startup-Vibe (gradient-as-identity, neon violet), Duolingo's gamified reward loops (streaks, badges, infantilizing copy), Anki's flat utilitarian legacy (a dense grid of gray controls from 2012), and cool dark modes built on purple-to-blue gradients.
keyCharacteristics: Dual register: Lora for reading surfaces, Inter for UI chrome, JetBrains Mono for metadata only
Warm terracotta primary on an ochre-tinted parchment field
Dual mode: warm light by default; dark mode is warm and receding, not electric
Shadows are ambient and diffuse, never structural or theatrical
Motion is quiet and responsive: state changes only, always ease-out, always reduced-motion safe

rules.name: The One Accent Rule
rules.body: Study Flame is never decorative. It marks active, focused, and primary-action states. On any given screen, no more than 10% of visible surface carries the accent. Its rarity is what makes it legible.

rules.name: The Warm Foundation Rule
rules.body: Every neutral is tinted toward ochre. Never #FFFFFF, never #000000. The warmth is in the substrate — both in light mode and dark mode.

rules.name: The Reader's Rule
rules.body: When text is meant to be read at length — summaries, chat responses, roadmap descriptions — Lora leads. When text is UI chrome — navigation, metadata, timestamps, button copy — Inter or JetBrains Mono do the work. Never invert this assignment.

rules.name: The Ambient-Only Rule
rules.body: Shadows define surfaces; they do not perform them. If a shadow reads louder than the content it holds, it has failed.

rules.name: The Tonal Layering Rule
rules.body: Depth inside reading panels comes from background steps (Warm Parchment → Reed Paper → Vellum), not additional shadow. Shadow is for the outer shell.

dos: Do use Study Flame for primary actions, active states, and focus indicators only. Its scarcity is its strength.
Do tint every neutral toward ochre. #FAFAF8 not #FFFFFF. #1C1917 not #000000.
Do use Lora for any text meant to be read at length: summaries, chat content, headings in reading views.
Do use JetBrains Mono exclusively for metadata: labels, file names, timestamps, source tags.
Do use ambient diffuse shadows. Two-layer: atmospheric + definition. Keep them quiet.
Do apply @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) to collapse all animations to 0ms.
Do confirm destructive actions (module deletion) with an explicit modal.
Do keep minimum visible text at 13px / 0.81rem.
Do use cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1) (ease-out) for all state transitions.
donts: Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). Use solid Study Flame or Ink instead.
Don't use border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored stripe on blockquotes, callouts, or list items. Use background tints. (Two current violations in summary-result.)
Don't use cubic-bezier(0.34,1.56,0.64,1) (ease-spring) — it produces a bounce. Replace with ease-out.
Don't produce an AI-Tech-Startup aesthetic: no gradient-as-brand, no neon or violet palette, no glowing accent fields.
Don't add gamification: no streaks, no badges, no progress percentages for their own sake.
Don't replicate the Anki or legacy study tool look: no dense grids of gray utility controls.
Don't add ambient decoration: no floating background words, no particle systems.
Don't use glassmorphism decoratively. Blurred backdrops for transient overlays only.
Don't nest cards inside cards. Use tonal background stepping for interior hierarchy.
Don't build dark mode by inverting light mode. Dark mode is its own warm palette.
